title: Introducing yourself
objectives: Ask someone their name and answer back
Know the difference between familiar 'tú' and formal 'usted'

vocab.hint: agrees with the speaker's gender — just like Russian

grammar_notes: Spanish has two 'you': tú (familiar, friends/family) and usted (formal, strangers/elders). Russian has the same split with ты/Вы.
Adjectives like encantado/encantada agree with the SPEAKER's gender, not the listener's.
example_phrases: — ¿Cómo te llamas? — Me llamo Lena. ¿Y tú?
— Soy Carlos. — Mucho gusto, Carlos. — Encantada.
